# @platform/config-runtime

Configuration service: environment variable loading, schema validation, typed config access, and secret resolution.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/config-runtime
Version: 0.1.0
Component: config-runtime
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: operations
Bounded context: config-service
Owner: team-platform
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: platform
Class: active.platform
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-platform
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: true
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Load and validate environment variables against a typed schema at process start
- Provide typed, readonly config accessors to other platform packages
- Resolve secret references from external secret stores (e.g. AWS Secrets Manager)
- Fail fast at startup if required config is absent or malformed

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not store or persist configuration in a database
- Does not expose config over HTTP or as a public API
- Does not own feature flags or runtime toggles

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- Imported by adapter and runtime packages to access their required configuration
- Call once at process start to validate and freeze the config object

## Operational notes

- Config is validated and frozen at startup ? mutation after boot is not supported
- Secret resolution may incur I/O latency on first access
